original_prompt: Hey there! I'm going to Tokyo next week. Can you do me a favor and tell me the address, rating and minimum price for 'City Hub'? If it's rated over 4 and the price is under 200, please add an event to my calendar on January 2nd 2025 to remind me about reserving the hotel? Use the hotel name as the title and its address as the location. I'll book a room later. Thanks a lot!
assessment_reasoning: The user intent is clear (get address, rating, minimum price for 'City Hub' and create a calendar reminder on 2025-01-02 if rating > 4 and price < 200). However several execution details are unspecified: no required format for the returned rating/price/address (which the verifier expects exact numeric substrings), no currency or whether price is per night vs total, no clarification whether “over 4” means strictly >4 or ≥4, and important calendar details are omitted (start/end times, all-day vs timed event, description text). Also no timezone or date-time format is specified. These missing specifics mean the agent must make assumptions about price units/currency and event start/end times/description, so the prompt is moderately ambiguous.

variations.L1: I'm heading to Tokyo next week. Can you do something useful with the hotel called City Hub? Thanks.
variations.L2: I'm going to Tokyo next week. Can you tell me City Hub's address, rating, and minimum price? If it looks good (rating over 4 and price under 200) please set a reminder for me to reserve the hotel.
variations.L3: Hey there! I'm going to Tokyo next week. Can you do me a favor and tell me the address, rating and minimum price for 'City Hub'? If it's rated over 4 and the price is under 200, please add an event to my calendar on January 2nd 2025 to remind me about reserving the hotel? Use the hotel name as the title and its address as the location. I'll book a room later. Thanks a lot!
variations.L4: Please retrieve City Hub's address, average rating, and minimum nightly price. If the rating is greater than 4.0 and the price is below 200 (USD), create a calendar event on 2025-01-02 titled "City Hub" with the event location set to the hotel's address. Make the event cover the whole day (start 2025-01-02 00:00, end 2025-01-02 23:59) and include the description: "Stay at City Hub hotel." Do not book anything — just set the reminder.
variations.L5: Action plan: 1) Call get_rating_reviews_for_hotels with hotel_names = ["City Hub"] and return the rating. 2) Call get_hotels_prices with hotel_names = ["City Hub"] and return the minimum price. 3) Call get_hotels_address with hotel_name = "City Hub" and return the address. 4) If the rating > 4.0 and the minimum price < 200, call create_calendar_event with: {"title": "City Hub", "start_time": "2025-01-02 00:00", "end_time": "2025-01-02 23:59", "description": "Stay at City Hub hotel.", "location": <address returned in step 3>}. After performing the steps, report the address, rating, and minimum price in your reply and confirm if the calendar event was created.
